title: Aureline spike-timing trace record
description: Boundary schema for the spike-timing trace record the shell spike (crates/aureline-shell-spike) emits for startup, first-paint, input-to-paint, render-submission, and render-adjacent hot paths so the benchmark lab, the journey harness, and later taxonomy work can consume a single structured record family without renaming fields. The record pairs the ADR-0002 protected-hot-path hook vocabulary (docs/adr/0002-renderer-text-stack-and-shaping-fallback.md §Protected-hot-path hook list) with the protected-path / journey-budget vocabulary frozen in docs/benchmarks/spike_metric_names.md; two hooks that ride the same protected_path measure against the same journey budget. Raw timer bodies, raw render-surface contents, and raw build material never cross this boundary; the record carries an opaque build identity, typed hook and protected-path tokens, provisional trace/span ids, and integer counters only. Adding a new protected_path value, a new counter field, a new build-identity field, or a new backend class is additive-minor and requires a schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

spike_timing_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this spike-timing trace export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

schema_token.description: Frozen schema discriminator. Every spike-timing trace carries schema = aureline.spike_timing.v1 so consumers that multiplex several trace families on one parser can route by field.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable id. Used for scene_id, fixture_ref, journey_id, trace_id, and span_id. No raw URLs, raw file paths, or raw symbol bodies cross this boundary.

hook_name.description: The ADR-0002 protected-hot-path hook vocabulary. Lifted verbatim from docs/adr/0002-renderer-text-stack-and-shaping-fallback.md §Protected-hot-path hook list. No trace emitter MAY invent synonyms.

protected_path_class.description: Frozen protected-path journey vocabulary from docs/benchmarks/spike_metric_names.md. Two hooks that share a protected_path measure against the same journey budget. first_useful_chrome, frame_budget, and placeholder_* are reserved for later lanes that are not yet exercised by the M0 spike; they remain admissible so later work wires in without a schema version bump.

backend_class.description: Frozen backend-selection vocabulary matching CapabilityManifest.Backend in the spike. headless runs emit traces and exit; native_window runs drive a winit-class window.

host_os_class.description: Frozen host-OS vocabulary. unknown covers build hosts that do not match the other three; it is admissible so the schema does not reject developer-local builds on niche hosts.

counter_value.description: Non-negative integer counter. Counters are deterministic per fixture-scene run in the M0 spike.

crate_name.description: Cargo crate name of the emitter. Usually aureline-shell-spike in the M0 spike; other crates that emit through this schema carry their own crate name.

crate_version.description: Crate version string embedded at compile time (CARGO_PKG_VERSION).

rustc_target_triple.description: Compact target triple as reported by the spike (apple-darwin, unknown-linux-gnu, pc-windows-msvc, unknown). The exact-build identity record in schemas/build/exact_build_identity.schema.json is the super-set this field hooks into; here the spike carries only the minimum it self-reports.

build_identity_record.description: Minimum build-identity hook carried on every trace so later taxonomy work can upgrade to the full exact-build identity record without renaming fields. See docs/build/exact_build_identity_model.md.

paint_count_by_zone.description: Paint count per spike render zone. The zone vocabulary mirrors src/zones.rs ZoneId and is narrower than the canonical shell-zone model.

paint_count_by_layer.description: Paint count per ADR-0002 scene layer. Caret, selection, and IME composition ride overlay; reflow, scroll, and scale-change ride text_and_decoration.

invalidation_class_counts.description: Mark count bucketed by protected-path invalidation class. first_useful_chrome, frame_budget, and placeholder_* are reserved in protected_path_class but do not appear as counter buckets until the M0 spike exercises them.

counters_record.description: Aggregate counters for one emitted trace. The M0 spike wires total_marks, hot_path_marks, damage_records, paint_count_by_zone, paint_count_by_layer, invalidation_class_counts, and visible_pane_work to real measurements; hidden_pane_work, frame_misses, offscreen_suppression_eligible, and fallback_glyph_resolutions are provisional zeros until later lanes exercise them (docs/benchmarks/spike_metric_names.md §Provisional counters).

protected_hot_path.description: True when the hook is on ADR-0002's protected hot-path list; false for observability-only hooks (atlas_eviction, degraded_renderer_banner).

path_id.description: Stable id for the hook the mark represents. In the M0 spike this is always the hook name verbatim so the id and the hook field stay mechanically aligned; later taxonomy work may widen path_id into a composite without renaming the field.

tick.description: Monotonic tick recorded by the timing recorder at the moment the hook fired. In the fixture scene this is a CountingClock tick (0..N) so committed samples stay byte-stable; in a wall-clock run this is nanoseconds since the binary started.

note.description: Free-form label carried over from the timing recorder. Not structured.

mark_record.description: One recorded hook firing. The (hook, tick) pair is the minimum the benchmark lab needs to compute latencies; protected_path, path_id, scenario_id, and span_id are the provisional seat for journey-harness and trace-context wiring.

marks.description: Ordered marks in arrival order. The compositor coalesces per zone per frame; the trace keeps the raw sequence so per-hook journey budgets are computable.

spike_timing_trace_record.description: One spike-timing trace record. Aggregate (full_scene) and per-label variants share this shape; per-label traces carry a single mark so the benchmark lab can check one hook in isolation.
